perturbation.original_text: 5.2.1 Invoicing and Payment. Operator shall invoice Owner, monthly, for all costs and expenses incurred hereunder during the immediately preceding month and, without duplication, in any prior month. Owner shall pay all such amounts prior to the later of (a) 50 days after the end of the month in which the expenses were incurred or (b) 10 days after the date of receipt of each invoice. Invoices received after 1:00 pm local time will be deemed received on the next Business Day. If the day on which any payment is due is not a Business Day, then the relevant payment shall be due upon the immediately succeeding Business Day. Any amounts which remain due and owing after the due date shall bear interest at the lower of the prime rate as published in the "Money Rates" section of The Wall Street Journal plus two percent (2%) per annum and the maximum lawful rate of interest, compounded daily from the date such payment is due until such payment is made. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, any indemnification payments required to be made by a Party hereunder shall be made to the indemnified Party upon receipt by the indemnifying Party of written demand therefor from the indemnified Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2.1 Invoicing and Payment. Operator shall invoice Owner, monthly, for all costs and expenses incurred hereunder during the immediately preceding month and, without duplication, in any prior month. Owner shall pay all such amounts prior to the earlier of (a) 50 days after the end of the month in which the expenses were incurred or (b) 10 days after the date of receipt of each invoice. Invoices received after 1:00 pm local time will be deemed received on the next Business Day. If the day on which any payment is due is not a Business Day, then the relevant payment shall be due upon the immediately succeeding Business Day. Any amounts which remain due and owing after the due date shall bear interest at the lower of the prime rate as published in the "Money Rates" section of The Wall Street Journal plus two percent (2%) per annum and the maximum lawful rate of interest, compounded daily from the date such payment is due until such payment is made. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, any indemnification payments required to be made by a Party hereunder shall be made to the indemnified Party upon receipt by the indemnifying Party of written demand therefor from the indemnified Party.
perturbation.explanation: The change from "the later of" to "the earlier of" creates an in-text contradiction. The original clause allowed Owner to pay based on whichever timeframe was longer (50 days after the month or 10 days after invoice), whereas the modified clause forces Owner to pay based on the shorter timeframe. This directly affects the payment terms outlined, because one term states the longer timeframe is permissible, and the other makes the shorter timeframe mandatory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.2.1 Invoicing and Payment. Operator shall invoice Owner, monthly, for all costs and expenses incurred hereunder during the immediately preceding month and, without duplication, in any prior month. Owner shall pay all such amounts prior to the earlier of (a) 50 days after the end of the month in which the expenses were incurred or (b) 10 days after the date of receipt of each invoice. Invoices received after 1:00 pm local time will be deemed received on the next Business Day. If the day on which any payment is due is not a Business Day, then the relevant payment shall be due upon the immediately succeeding Business Day. Any amounts which remain due and owing after the due date shall bear interest at the lower of the prime rate as published in the "Money Rates" section of The Wall Street Journal plus two percent (2%) per annum and the maximum lawful rate of interest, compounded daily from the date such payment is due until such payment is made. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, any indemnification payments required to be made by a Party hereunder shall be made to the indemnified Party upon receipt by the indemnifying Party of written demand therefor from the indemnified Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment terms from 'the later of' to 'the earlier of' (a) 50 days after the end of the month in which the expenses were incurred or (b) 10 days after the date of receipt of each invoice. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, meaning that in effect the entire clause now states the opposite of what it originally stated. This creates a direct and irreconcilable contradiction within the payment terms, as the Owner is now required to pay based on the shorter timeframe. According to the definition for 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', this is a 'YES' because the different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and, unless terminated earlier pursuant to Section 3.2 or 3.3 of this Agreement, shall continue until the twentieth (20th) anniversary of the Effective Date; provided, however, (i) Operator shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at any time upon no less than 120 days prior written notice to Owner, if Operator provides a substitute operator acceptable to Owner, as determined in its reasonable discretion, who (A) has experience operating similar assets, (B) has the ability to provide at least the same quality of service as Operator, (C) has the financial ability to perform the obligations hereunder, and (D) is ready, willing and able to execute an operation and maintenance agreement substantially similar to this Agreement and acceptable to Owner, as determined in its reasonable discretion, and (ii) Owner shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at any time upon no less than 120 days prior written notice to Operator.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and, unless terminated earlier pursuant to Section 3.2 or 3.3 of this Agreement, shall continue until the twentieth (20th) anniversary of the Effective Date; provided, however, (i) Operator shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at any time upon no less than 120 days prior written notice to Owner, if Operator provides a substitute operator acceptable to Owner, as determined in its sole discretion, who (A) has experience operating similar assets, (B) has the ability to provide at least the same quality of service as Operator, (C) has the financial ability to perform the obligations hereunder, and (D) is ready, willing and able to execute an operation and maintenance agreement substantially similar to this Agreement and acceptable to Owner, as determined in its reasonable discretion, and (ii) Owner shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at any time upon no less than 30 days prior written notice to Operator.
perturbation.explanation: The change from "reasonable discretion" to "sole discretion" in part (i) gives the operator the freedom to determine in what way a sub operator is acceptable to owner, while the change from "120 days prior written notice" to "30 days prior written notice" to Operator in part (ii) creates an in-text contradiction. The original clause made it so that the Owner has to give a 120 day written notice, and the modified text, gives the Owner 30 days to provide written notice. This is contradicting because a earlier clause states that 120 days are needed for proper notice of termination, but the modified text undermines that specific term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and, unless terminated earlier pursuant to Section 3.2 or 3.3 of this Agreement, shall continue until the twentieth (20th) anniversary of the Effective Date; provided, however, (i) Operator shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at any time upon no less than 120 days prior written notice to Owner, if Operator provides a substitute operator acceptable to Owner, as determined in its sole discretion, who (A) has experience operating similar assets, (B) has the ability to provide at least the same quality of service as Operator, (C) has the financial ability to perform the obligations hereunder, and (D) is ready, willing and able to execute an operation and maintenance agreement substantially similar to this Agreement and acceptable to Owner, as determined in its reasonable discretion, and (ii) Owner shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at any time upon no less than 30 days prior written notice to Operator.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text mandates a 120-day notice period for termination by the Owner. The changed text modifies this requirement, stipulating a 30-day notice period for Owner termination. This directly contradicts the initial term of 120 days, creating an inconsistency within the contract regarding the notice period required for termination, satisfying the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1.2 To the extent of the liabilities assumed by Operator in this Agreement, Operator shall name Owner Indemnified Parties as additional insured on all insurance policies, except Workers' Compensation. The Owner Indemnified Parties' additional insured status will not limit the application of insurance protection as required by this Agreement which arises out of the Operator's indemnity obligations. These policies shall provide primary coverage for claims in which Operator has agreed to hold harmless and/or to indemnify the Owner Indemnified Parties. No "other insurance" clause may be invoked by any insurer. This coverage shall apply whether or not the indemnification is valid. Operator shall have its insurer(s) waive its right of subrogation against Owner Indemnified Parties on all insurance carried. Unless expressly stated to the contrary elsewhere in this Agreement or prohibited by applicable law or legal statute, Operator's indemnification obligations under this Agreement shall not be limited by amount or in scope to coverage provided by insurance which is required under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1.2 To the extent of the liabilities assumed by Operator in this Agreement, Operator shall name Owner Indemnified Parties as additional insured on all insurance policies, except Workers' Compensation. The Owner Indemnified Parties' additional insured status will not limit the application of insurance protection as required by this Agreement which arises out of the Operator's indemnity obligations. These policies shall provide secondary coverage for claims in which Operator has agreed to hold harmless and/or to indemnify the Owner Indemnified Parties. No "other insurance" clause may be invoked by any insurer. This coverage shall apply whether or not the indemnification is valid. Operator shall have its insurer(s) waive its right of subrogation against Owner Indemnified Parties on all insurance carried. Unless expressly stated to the contrary elsewhere in this Agreement or prohibited by applicable law or legal statute, Operator's indemnification obligations under this Agreement shall not be limited by amount or in scope to coverage provided by insurance which is required under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change from "primary coverage" to "secondary coverage" for the Owner Indemnified Parties creates an in-text contradiction regarding the extent of insurance protection provided. The clause previously stipulated that the insurance coverage should be the first line of defense for claims, but now it implies that other insurance policies should be exhausted before this coverage is applied. This change reduces the immediate protection for the Owner Indemnified Parties and introduces ambiguity about which insurance policy should respond first in the event of a claim. It is contradicting to the other sections where it states the primary protection is provided
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1.2 To the extent of the liabilities assumed by Operator in this Agreement, Operator shall name Owner Indemnified Parties as additional insured on all insurance policies, except Workers' Compensation. The Owner Indemnified Parties' additional insured status will not limit the application of insurance protection as required by this Agreement which arises out of the Operator's indemnity obligations. These policies shall provide secondary coverage for claims in which Operator has agreed to hold harmless and/or to indemnify the Owner Indemnified Parties. No "other insurance" clause may be invoked by any insurer. This coverage shall apply whether or not the indemnification is valid. Operator shall have its insurer(s) waive its right of subrogation against Owner Indemnified Parties on all insurance carried. Unless expressly stated to the contrary elsewhere in this Agreement or prohibited by applicable law or legal statute, Operator's indemnification obligations under this Agreement shall not be limited by amount or in scope to coverage provided by insurance which is required under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'primary coverage' to 'secondary coverage' directly contradicts the original intent of the clause, which was to provide the Owner Indemnified Parties with the first line of defense in insurance claims. The changed text now implies that other insurance policies must be exhausted before this coverage applies, diminishing the immediate protection and creating ambiguity regarding which policy responds first. The contradictory text is the same text location. Therefore, it is a strong in-text contradiction.
